user.title: Select Connection Type
user.description: Choose whether you want to connect through Bluetooth or an IDS CAN-to-Ethernet bridge.

user_ble.title: Select OneControl Gateway
user_ble.description: Choose a discovered OneControl BLE gateway to set up.

ethernet.title: Configure CAN-to-Ethernet Bridge
ethernet.description: If the bridge was discovered over UDP, the fields are prefilled. Otherwise enter them manually. Typical defaults are {default_host}:{default_port}.

pairing_method.title: Gateway Type — {name}
pairing_method.description:
Does your OneControl gateway have a physical **Connect** button you press to start pairing, or does it use a fixed PIN printed on a sticker?

Check the label on your gateway if you're unsure.

confirm.title: Configure {name}
confirm.description: Enter the 6-digit PIN from the sticker on your OneControl gateway.

confirm_pin.title: Configure {name} (PIN Gateway)
confirm_pin.description:
This is a legacy PIN-based gateway. Your gateway PIN is used for both Bluetooth bonding and protocol authentication.

**Note:** PIN gateways work best with a direct USB Bluetooth adapter. ESPHome Bluetooth Proxy support is untested and may not work.

data.bluetooth_pin: Bluetooth PIN override (leave blank to use Gateway PIN)

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the bridge endpoint

abort.no_devices_found: No OneControl gateways found. Make sure Bluetooth is enabled and the gateway is powered on.

init.title: External Device Naming
init.description: Optional: provide OneControl manifest/snapshot JSON content (paste from diagnostics export) or local file paths to improve naming when gateway metadata is unavailable. Pasted JSON takes priority over path input.

error.invalid_naming_file: Unable to read or parse the supplied manifest/snapshot file
